<!-- Generated from metadata.json + impl-plan.json — DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY -->
# semantic-dup を活用した DRY 違反の自動検出 capability

## Summary

This track implements the DRY violation auto-detection capability by building on the existing semantic-dup commands and the review system analogue. The work is decomposed into five hexagonal layers following the existing port/adapter/interactor pattern: domain types+ports (T001), usecase service ports+errors+judgment types (T002), infrastructure persistence adapter + diff-source adapter + commit-hash store adapter (T003), usecase interactors for the three operation paths (T004-T005), infrastructure agent adapter (T006), CLI wiring for all three sotp dry subcommands (T007-T009), and configuration/workflow registration (T010). The hexagonal execution order is T001 (domain) -> T002 (usecase ports) -> T003 (infra adapters, depends on T001 and T002) -> T004-T005 (interactors) -> T006 (agent adapter) -> T007-T009 (CLI) -> T010 (config).
The pair identity model uses FragmentRef — a (path, content_hash) compound identifier. A FragmentRef encapsulates both a repo-relative FilePath and a FragmentContentHash (SHA-256 hex of the fragment's content). Two FragmentRefs are equal iff BOTH path AND content_hash match — this is the self-match criterion. DryCheckPairKey holds two FragmentRefs sorted lexicographically by (path, content_hash) order into (low, high), ensuring (X,Y) and (Y,X) produce the same key (CN-08). DryCheckPairKey::new(a, b) is FALLIBLE: it rejects self-match (a == b on both path AND content_hash) with Err(SelfMatch). Note: paths-different-hash-same (complete copies in different files) is NOT a self-match and produces a valid pair. Invalidation is identifier-based (CN-07): when a fragment's content changes, its FragmentContentHash changes, so the FragmentRef changes, so the DryCheckPairKey changes, so no existing record matches — no separate hash-comparison step is needed. The interactor computes FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of CodeFragment.content() for each fragment and constructs FragmentRef::new(path, content_hash) before calling DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref, candidate_ref). The on-disk schema stores the pair as four flat fields: low_path, low_hash, high_path, high_hash — the FsDryCheckStore DTO reconstructs FragmentRef::new(low_path, low_hash) and FragmentRef::new(high_path, high_hash) then calls DryCheckPairKey::new(low_ref, high_ref) on deserialization.
The write/read separation: DryCheckEntry (interactor-constructed write-input, 7 fields, no recorded_at) vs DryCheckRecord (infra-constructed read/persistent form, mirrors DryCheckEntry plus recorded_at: Timestamp). DryCheckEntry::new is fallible (validates changed_path in pair; returns Err(DryCheckEntryError::ChangedPathOutsidePair)). DryCheckRecord holds all DryCheckEntry fields plus recorded_at: Timestamp (stamped by FsDryCheckStore at write time). DryCheckVerdict is enum-first: Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } — empty-proposal state structurally unrepresentable; NotAViolation and Accepted are unit variants. Rationale is a validated non-empty newtype (rationale() -> &Rationale); RefactorProposal is a validated non-empty newtype (refactor_proposal.as_str()). Timestamp is the existing domain::timestamp::Timestamp (action: reference — NOT a new definition). DryCheckFinding carries changed_fragment_ref: FragmentRef, candidate_fragment_ref: FragmentRef, and refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal; this is the live write-path finding returned to dfl. The agent adapter (T006) computes FragmentRefs from the actual CodeFragment arguments passed to judge() and constructs Rationale/RefactorProposal from the agent's text output — empty rationale or empty proposal maps to IllegalOutput — upholding D8/D9/CN-07: identity data (path, content_hash) is always derived from real fragment content, not from LLM output.
The three sotp dry operation paths from D10 (write / results / check-approved) map to DryCheckService / DryCheckResultsService / DryCheckApprovalService respectively. DryCheckService.run_dry_check accepts corpus_fragments, diff_fragments, threshold, AND base_commit (4 params). DryCheckApprovalService.check_approved accepts corpus_fragments, diff_fragments, and threshold (3 params, no base_commit). The gate predicate in check_approved is a two-step identifier-based check per candidate pair: (1) construct DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref, candidate_ref) — Err(SelfMatch) => skip; Ok(pair_key) => proceed; (2) look up pair_key in the latest-per-pair map (key = record.pair_key() directly, the already-sorted DryCheckPairKey with FragmentRefs); if absent: unresolved (unverified); if present: check verdict matches!(DryCheckVerdict::NotAViolation | DryCheckVerdict::Accepted) — Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } counts as unresolved. No separate hash-comparison step (invalidation is implicit in the identifier — if content changed, the FragmentRefs changed, the DryCheckPairKey changed, the lookup fails). DryCheckInteractor has a 5-param constructor — diff_source NOT injected. The interactor builds DryCheckEntry (7 fields, no recorded_at) and calls DryCheckWriter::append_record(&entry) — the infra adapter (FsDryCheckStore) stamps Timestamp internally; the interactor never produces a Timestamp. DryCheckCycleError::Entry wraps DryCheckEntryError (the old Record variant is replaced). CN-01 enforced end-to-end: both sotp dry write and check-approved use dry-check-owned adapters. The diff acquisition pipeline in both commands: list_changed_hunks -> extract_code_fragments -> fragments_overlapping_hunks -> diff_fragments (hunk-scoped per D4/CN-04). Diff-base resolution in both commands applies a fail-closed three-branch policy (mirroring review_v2::resolve_diff_base / D4): FsDryCheckCommitHashStore::read() Ok(Some(hash)) -> use it; Ok(None) (absent or non-ancestor) -> fall back to git rev-parse main; Err(DryCheckCommitHashError::Format) -> eprintln! warn then fall back to git rev-parse main (absorbed by the resolution layer — never surfaced as CLI error; a malformed .commit_hash must not abort the gate). --base-commit overrides this lookup when provided.
T001 has no dependencies and is implemented first: it defines RefactorProposal (non-empty newtype) + Rationale (non-empty newtype) + FragmentRef + FragmentContentHash + DryCheckPairKey (FragmentRef-based) + DryCheckEntry (7 fields, 7-arg fallible constructor, no recorded_at) + DryCheckRecord (read form, mirrors Entry + Timestamp) + DryCheckFinding (FragmentRef-based, refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal) + hunk-scope types (DiffHunkRange, DiffFileHunks) + CodeFragment extension + fragments_overlapping_hunks, plus the DryCheckReader/DryCheckWriter secondary-port traits (DryCheckWriter::append_record takes &DryCheckEntry). Timestamp is referenced (not defined) from domain::timestamp::Timestamp. T002 (usecase ports/skeleton) depends on T001 only. T003 (infrastructure adapters) depends on BOTH T001 (for domain types DryCheckEntry, DryCheckRecord, DryCheckPairKey, FragmentRef, Rationale, RefactorProposal, DiffFileHunks, and the DryCheckReader/DryCheckWriter port traits that FsDryCheckStore implements) AND T002 (for the usecase DryCheckDiffSource port trait that GitDryCheckDiffGetter implements). This ordering — T001 domain, T002 usecase, T003 infra — ensures each task compiles against already-defined types and traits. T004-T005 depend on T002 (usecase ports). T006 depends on T002 (usecase ports). T007-T009 depend on T004-T006. T010 depends on T007-T009. Tasks T001-T003 each target <400 lines of Rust; T003 covers three adapters; T004-T006 each target <400 lines; T007-T009 each target <300 lines; T010 is config+doc only.
The D8/IN-06 candidate enumeration loop in T004 and T005 uses a growing-k threshold-boundary strategy. The loop grows k (k, 2k, 4k, ...) and stops only when the returned batch contains at least one candidate where score.value() < threshold.value() (threshold boundary crossed), when the batch is empty, or when fewer than k results are returned (index exhausted). Self-matches are excluded by DryCheckPairKey::new before agent invocation. Stopping when 'all candidates are already verified' is explicitly NOT a termination condition.
The CodexDryChecker error taxonomy (T006) mirrors CodexReviewer. timed_out=true -> Timeout; exit_success=false -> AgentAbort; exit_success=true with missing output / parse failure / empty-rationale / empty-proposal -> IllegalOutput. The agent JSON schema does not include fragment_ref fields — the adapter computes FragmentRefs from the real CodeFragment arguments, so there is no content_hash parsing from agent output and therefore no invalid-hash-format -> IllegalOutput path for T006. DryCheckAgentError::UserAbort is declared for completeness.

## Tasks (5/10 resolved)

### S1 — Domain layer — dry_check value types, ports, and errors

> Add the domain/dry_check module containing all value objects: RefactorProposal (non-empty newtype; constructor RefactorProposal::new(s: impl Into<String>) -> Result<RefactorProposal, RefactorProposalError> rejects empty with Err(RefactorProposalError::Empty); accessor as_str() -> &str), RefactorProposalError (single variant: Empty), Rationale (non-empty newtype; constructor Rationale::new(s: impl Into<String>) -> Result<Rationale, RationaleError> rejects empty with Err(RationaleError::Empty); accessor as_str() -> &str), RationaleError (single variant: Empty), FragmentContentHash (SHA-256 hex, 64 lowercase chars, validated at construction, Ord+Hash), FragmentRef (private fields path: FilePath and content_hash: FragmentContentHash; infallible constructor FragmentRef::new; accessors path() -> &FilePath and content_hash() -> &FragmentContentHash; Ord by (path, content_hash) lexicographic order; derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash), DryCheckPairKey (private sorted FragmentRef pair; FALLIBLE constructor DryCheckPairKey::new(a, b) -> Result<_, DryCheckPairKeyError>: sorts by (path, content_hash) order, rejects self-match when BOTH path AND content_hash match; accessors low() -> &FragmentRef and high() -> &FragmentRef), DryCheckPairKeyError (single variant: SelfMatch), DryCheckVerdict (enum-first: NotAViolation { unit }, Accepted { unit }, Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } — empty-proposal state structurally unrepresentable), VerdictFilter (All/NotAViolation/Accepted/Violation), DryCheckEntry (write-input type; 7 required fields: pair_key, changed_path, verdict, similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale: Rationale; NO recorded_at field; FALLIBLE constructor DryCheckEntry::new with 7 arguments; validates changed_path equals pair_key.low().path() or pair_key.high().path(); accessors for all 7 fields returning typed values including rationale() -> &Rationale; derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq — Eq NOT derived because SimilarityScore(f32) and SimilarityThreshold(f32) fields make Eq underivable), DryCheckEntryError (ChangedPathOutsidePair), DryCheckRecord (read/persistent form; fields mirror DryCheckEntry plus recorded_at: Timestamp; constructed exclusively by FsDryCheckStore::append_record; accessors including rationale() -> &Rationale and recorded_at() -> &Timestamp; derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq — Eq NOT derived for the same reason as DryCheckEntry), DryCheckRecordError (ChangedPathOutsidePair — retained for infra-internal constructor completeness), Timestamp (reference to existing domain::timestamp::Timestamp — action: reference, NOT a new type), DryCheckFinding (changed_fragment_ref: FragmentRef, candidate_fragment_ref: FragmentRef, refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal; constructor DryCheckFinding::new calls RefactorProposal::new internally; accessors changed_fragment_ref() -> &FragmentRef, candidate_fragment_ref() -> &FragmentRef, refactor_proposal() -> &RefactorProposal), DryCheckFindingError (EmptyProposal), DryCheckApprovalVerdict (Approved/Blocked{unresolved_pair_count}), DryCheckReaderError, DryCheckWriterError; DryCheckReader/DryCheckWriter secondary-port traits. DryCheckWriter has ONE method: append_record(&self, entry: &DryCheckEntry) — receives DryCheckEntry (7 fields, no recorded_at); infra adapter stamps Timestamp internally. Also add hunk-scope types: DiffHunkRange, DiffHunkRangeError, DiffFileHunks, DiffFileHunksError. Modify CodeFragment: add start_line/end_line fields and accessors. Implement fragments_overlapping_hunks pure helper.
> Identifier-based invalidation (CN-07): when fragment content changes, content_hash changes, FragmentRef changes, DryCheckPairKey changes, no existing record matches — no separate hash-comparison step. Self-match criterion: BOTH path AND content_hash must be equal (paths different but hashes same = two distinct complete-copy files = valid pair NOT a self-match). DryCheckFinding.changed_fragment_ref / candidate_fragment_ref are the full (path, content_hash) identifiers of the two fragments (informational on the finding; also used by the interactor to derive the DryCheckPairKey). Write/read separation: DryCheckEntry is the write-input (interactor constructs it — 7 fields, no recorded_at); DryCheckRecord is the persistent read form (infra stamps Timestamp and builds it). DryCheckWriter::append_record has init-on-first-write contract. Derive note: DryCheckEntry and DryCheckRecord both embed SimilarityScore(f32) and SimilarityThreshold(f32) — Eq is NOT derivable for either type (f32 does not implement Eq); PartialEq is derived instead and is sufficient. As a prerequisite, domain::semantic_dup::SimilarityScore and SimilarityThreshold must have PartialEq added (action: modify on existing types) so that DryCheckEntry/DryCheckRecord can derive PartialEq.

Implement DryCheckRecord struct (read/persistent form; constructed exclusively by FsDryCheckStore::append_record which stamps Timestamp; private fields mirror DryCheckEntry plus recorded_at: Timestamp; accessors pair_key() -> &DryCheckPairKey, changed_path() -> &FilePath, verdict() -> &DryCheckVerdict, similarity_score() -> &SimilarityScore, threshold() -> &SimilarityThreshold, base_commit() -> &CommitHash, rationale() -> &Rationale, recorded_at() -> &Timestamp; derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq — Eq NOT derived because SimilarityScore(f32) and SimilarityThreshold(f32) fields make Eq underivable; PartialEq is sufficient for all tests). (which returns Result<domain::Timestamp, domain::ValidationError>) to obtain a Timestamp — no Timestamp::new re-wrap; the interactor never produces a Timestamp. Also implement hunk-scope support (D4): DiffHunkRange struct (private start_line: u32, end_line: u32; 1-indexed inclusive line range; FALLIBLE constructor DiffHunkRange::new(start_line: u32, end_line: u32) -> Result<DiffHunkRange, DiffHunkRangeError>: rejects zero-line numbers (DiffHunkRangeError::ZeroLine) and inverted ranges start > end (DiffHunkRangeError::StartExceedsEnd { start, end }); accessors start_line() -> u32 and end_line() -> u32), DiffHunkRangeError enum (two variants: StartExceedsEnd { start: u32, end: u32 } and ZeroLine), DiffFileHunks struct (private path: FilePath, hunks: Vec<DiffHunkRange>; FALLIBLE constructor DiffFileHunks::new(path: FilePath, hunks: Vec<DiffHunkRange>) -> Result<DiffFileHunks, DiffFileHunksError>: rejects empty hunks (DiffFileHunksError::EmptyHunks); accessors path() -> &FilePath and hunks() -> &[DiffHunkRange]), DiffFileHunksError enum (single variant: EmptyHunks). For ad-hoc query fragments (source_path = '<query>'), start_line=1 / end_line=u32::MAX is used as a sentinel so query fragments are never excluded by hunk filtering. The FragmentContentHash (SHA-256 of content) is computed by the interactor from CodeFragment.content() at DryCheckPairKey construction time — it is NOT stored in CodeFragment itself. Implement domain helper function fragments_overlapping_hunks(fragments: &[CodeFragment], changed_hunks: &[DiffFileHunks]) -> Vec<CodeFragment> in domain::dry_check. Wire into domain::lib.rs. Unit tests: RefactorProposal::new rejects empty string (Err(RefactorProposalError::Empty)), accepts non-empty; Rationale::new rejects empty string (Err(RationaleError::Empty)), accepts non-empty; FragmentContentHash::new rejects non-64-hex; FragmentRef Ord (sorts by (path, content_hash)); DryCheckPairKey::new normalizes (Y,X)==(X,Y) using (path, content_hash) order and returns Err(SelfMatch) when both path AND content_hash match (not when only path matches); DryCheckEntry::new round-trips all 7 fields (pair_key(), changed_path(), verdict(), similarity_score(), threshold(), base_commit(), rationale()) and returns Err(ChangedPathOutsidePair) when changed_path is neither pair_key.low().path() nor pair_key.high().path(); DryCheckRecord (Timestamp stamped, accessible via recorded_at() -> &Timestamp) round-trip; DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } carries the non-empty proposal (empty-proposal state structurally unrepresentable); DryCheckFinding::new rejects empty proposal (EmptyProposal) and accepts FragmentRef for both fragment ref fields; DiffHunkRange::new rejects start=0 (ZeroLine) and start > end (StartExceedsEnd); DiffFileHunks::new rejects empty hunks (EmptyHunks); fragments_overlapping_hunks overlap tests. cargo make ci-rust passes. ### S2 — Usecase layer — application service traits, ports, judgment type, and error types

> Add the usecase/dry_check module skeleton: DryCheckAgentJudgment enum with struct variants (NotAViolation { rationale: Rationale }, Accepted { rationale: Rationale }, Violation { rationale: Rationale, finding: DryCheckFinding } — every variant carries Rationale (non-empty newtype); DryCheckFinding only when Violation; empty rationale structurally unrepresentable via Rationale::new), DryCheckAgentPort trait (judge receives CodeFragment for both sides; interactor computes FragmentRefs internally), DryCheckAgentError (UserAbort/AgentAbort/Timeout/IllegalOutput/Unexpected), DryCheckDiffError (Failed(String) — CN-01), DryCheckCycleError (Embedding/Index/Agent/Reader/Writer/Diff/Entry — Entry wraps DryCheckEntryError; the old Record variant is replaced by Entry because the interactor now constructs DryCheckEntry not DryCheckRecord; SelfMatch is a skip signal not a cycle error), DryCheckResults query struct (records: Vec<DryCheckRecord> with pair_key() as DryCheckPairKey containing two FragmentRefs, changed_path() display-only, verdict() including DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal }, similarity_score(), threshold(), base_commit(), rationale() -> &Rationale, recorded_at() -> &Timestamp), and the four application-service/port trait definitions.

DryCheckService.run_dry_check(corpus_fragments, diff_fragments, threshold, base_commit) — 4 params. DryCheckApprovalService.check_approved(corpus_fragments, diff_fragments, threshold) — 3 params, no base_commit. DryCheckResultsService.get_results(filter). every variant carries Rationale so entry.rationale() is always populated per D9/AC-06; an empty rationale string is structurally unrepresentable because Rationale::new rejects empty strings); DryCheckAgentError enum (UserAbort/AgentAbort/Timeout/IllegalOutput/Unexpected(String)); ### S3 — Infrastructure layer — FsDryCheckStore, GitDryCheckDiffGetter, and FsDryCheckCommitHashStore adapters

> Implement all three dry-check infrastructure adapters. FsDryCheckStore: sole filesystem adapter for dry-check.json. Atomic write, fs4 file locking, symlink rejection (CN-09), schema version gate. Hexagonal principle: domain types (DryCheckEntry, DryCheckRecord, DryCheckPairKey, FragmentRef, Rationale, RefactorProposal) are serde-free — all serde derives live on private infra-layer codec types only. Two private codec types: DryCheckRecordDto (flat per-record struct with four identifier string fields low_path, low_hash, high_path, high_hash — the two FragmentRefs flattened into four siblings; no nested pair_key object on disk; plus changed_path, verdict as DryCheckVerdictDto, similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale: String, recorded_at: String) and DryCheckJsonV1 { schema_version: u32, records: Vec<DryCheckRecordDto> }. append_record(entry: &DryCheckEntry) (NOT &DryCheckRecord): calls infrastructure::timestamp_now()? (returns Result<domain::Timestamp, domain::ValidationError>) to obtain a Timestamp — no Timestamp::new re-wrap — first; builds DryCheckRecordDto by reading from entry (pair_key().low().path() as low_path, pair_key().low().content_hash().as_str() as low_hash, pair_key().high().path() as high_path, pair_key().high().content_hash().as_str() as high_hash, entry.changed_path(), entry.verdict() -> DryCheckVerdictDto with Violation carrying entry's RefactorProposal.as_str(), entry.similarity_score(), entry.threshold(), entry.base_commit(), entry.rationale().as_str()) and sets dto.recorded_at = stamped_timestamp.as_str(); FsDryCheckStore::append_record is the SOLE owner of recorded_at — the interactor passes DryCheckEntry which has NO recorded_at field. Dto->domain (read path): FragmentContentHash::new(low_hash)?; FragmentRef::new(FilePath(low_path), low_content_hash); same for high; DryCheckPairKey::new(low_ref, high_ref)? (rejects self-match); for Violation verdict reconstruct RefactorProposal::new(refactor_proposal_str)?; Rationale::new(rationale_str)?; Timestamp::new(recorded_at_str)?; reconstruct DryCheckRecord with typed Rationale and Timestamp; any Err maps to DryCheckReaderError::InvalidData.
> GitDryCheckDiffGetter (CN-01: dry-check's own diff-source adapter): unit struct implementing DryCheckDiffSource (defined in T002/usecase). list_changed_hunks mirrors GitDiffGetter's 4-source union but extracts hunk-level line ranges; returns Vec<DiffFileHunks>. FsDryCheckCommitHashStore (CN-01): read() reports precisely with three outcomes: file absent -> Ok(None); invalid hash format -> Err(DryCheckCommitHashError::Format); non-ancestor -> Ok(None). The CLI-composition resolution layer (T007/T009) applies the fail-closed policy on top of these outcomes: Ok(Some(hash)) -> use it; Ok(None) -> fall back to git rev-parse main; Err(Format) -> eprintln! warn then fall back to git rev-parse main (absorbed by the resolution layer, never surfaced as CLI error — mirroring review_v2::resolve_diff_base). read() itself is unchanged. These three adapters share no interface with review_v2 gate interfaces (CN-01). T003 depends on T001 (domain types including DryCheckEntry, DryCheckRecord, Rationale, RefactorProposal, and the DryCheckReader/DryCheckWriter port traits) AND T002 (DryCheckDiffSource trait that GitDryCheckDiffGetter implements).

- [x] **T003**: Add infrastructure/dry_check module: implement FsDryCheckStore (atomic write + fs4 file locking, mirrors FsReviewStore). FsDryCheckStore::new(path: PathBuf, trusted_root: PathBuf). The on-disk format is a schema-versioned envelope object { schema_version: u32, records: [...] } mirroring ReviewJsonV2 { schema_version: u32, scopes: ... } from FsReviewStore. Hexagonal principle: domain types (DryCheckRecord, DryCheckEntry, DryCheckPairKey, FragmentRef) are serde-free. All serde derives live exclusively on private infra-layer codec types (following the FsReviewStore / ReviewJsonV2 DTO pattern). The on-disk per-record layout uses FLAT 4-identifier fields: low_path: String, low_hash: String, high_path: String, high_hash: String — these four fields together encode the two FragmentRefs that form the DryCheckPairKey. Concretely: (1) Define a private per-record DTO struct DryCheckRecordDto (serde only, never exposed outside this module) with fully flat fields matching the on-disk schema: low_path: String, low_hash: String, high_path: String, high_hash: String (the four identifiers from the two FragmentRefs), changed_path: String, verdict: DryCheckVerdictDto, similarity_score: f64, threshold: f64, base_commit: String, rationale: String, recorded_at: String. DryCheckVerdictDto: NotAViolation and Accepted serialize as bare string tags; Violation { refactor_proposal: String } serializes as a tagged object. (2) Define a private envelope codec struct DryCheckJsonV1 { schema_version: u32, records: Vec<DryCheckRecordDto> }. (3) DryCheckWriter::append_record(entry: &DryCheckEntry) (NOT &DryCheckRecord — the infra adapter receives DryCheckEntry from the interactor): call infrastructure::timestamp_now()? (returns Result<domain::Timestamp, domain::ValidationError>) to obtain a Timestamp — no Timestamp::new re-wrap — to produce a DryCheckRecord from the entry + Timestamp; build DryCheckRecordDto by reading from the entry: entry.pair_key().low().path() as low_path, entry.pair_key().low().content_hash().as_str() as low_hash, entry.pair_key().high().path() as high_path, entry.pair_key().high().content_hash().as_str() as high_hash — the DryCheckPairKey is flattened into four sibling string fields on disk; entry.changed_path(), entry.verdict() -> DryCheckVerdictDto (Violation variant carries entry.verdict() refactor_proposal.as_str()), entry.similarity_score(), entry.threshold(), entry.base_commit(), entry.rationale().as_str(); set dto.recorded_at to the freshly stamped Timestamp.as_str() — FsDryCheckStore::append_record is the SOLE owner of recorded_at; the interactor passes DryCheckEntry which has no recorded_at field; init-on-first-write, lock, read envelope under lock returning empty DryCheckJsonV1 when file absent, append, write atomically. (4) Dto -> domain conversion (read/deserialize path): from a DryCheckRecordDto, reconstruct: FragmentContentHash::new(low_hash)?, FilePath(low_path) -> FragmentRef::new(low_file_path, low_content_hash); same for high; then DryCheckPairKey::new(low_ref, high_ref)? (fallible — rejects self-match where both path AND content_hash match); verdict: for Violation reconstruct RefactorProposal::new(refactor_proposal)? to produce DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal }; Rationale::new(rationale)? to produce typed Rationale; Timestamp::new(recorded_at)? to produce typed Timestamp; reconstruct DryCheckRecord from pair_key, changed_path, verdict, similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale: Rationale, recorded_at: Timestamp; Err from any step (invalid hash format, self-match, empty proposal, empty rationale, invalid timestamp, changed_path outside pair) maps to DryCheckReaderError::InvalidData. Implement DryCheckReader::read_records (symlink check, parse envelope, schema version gate, convert each Dto to domain). Also implement GitDryCheckDiffGetter (CN-01: dry-check's own adapter): implements usecase::dry_check::DryCheckDiffSource (the DryCheckDiffSource trait is defined in T002/usecase, which T003/infra depends on); list_changed_hunks(&self, base: &CommitHash) -> Result<Vec<DiffFileHunks>, DryCheckDiffError>; mirrors GitDiffGetter (4-source union via SystemGitRepo) but extracts hunk-level line ranges; returns Vec<DiffFileHunks> carrying FilePath and non-empty Vec<DiffHunkRange>. Also implement FsDryCheckCommitHashStore (CN-01): new(path: PathBuf, trusted_root: PathBuf); read(&self) -> Result<Option<CommitHash>, DryCheckCommitHashError>; three outcomes: file absent -> Ok(None); invalid hash format -> Err(DryCheckCommitHashError::Format); non-ancestor -> Ok(None). Wire all three types into infrastructure::dry_check module. T003 depends on T001 (domain types: DryCheckEntry, DryCheckRecord, DryCheckPairKey, FragmentRef, Rationale, RefactorProposal, DiffFileHunks, and the DryCheckReader/DryCheckWriter port traits) AND T002 (usecase: the DryCheckDiffSource port trait that GitDryCheckDiffGetter implements). Tests: round-trip append_record(entry)/read_records via the full DryCheckEntry -> Dto -> JSON -> Dto -> DryCheckRecord pipeline (verify all fields survive — pair_key restored via FragmentRef::new(low_path, low_hash) + FragmentRef::new(high_path, high_hash) + DryCheckPairKey::new(low_ref, high_ref), pair_key().low().path()/.content_hash() and pair_key().high().path()/.content_hash() accessible; changed_path, verdict (with RefactorProposal for Violation), similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale as Rationale, recorded_at as Timestamp); first append on missing file creates file (init-on-first-write); read on missing file returns empty Vec; symlink rejection; DryCheckReaderError::IncompatibleSchema on future schema_version; recorded_at stamped by append_record via infrastructure::timestamp_now() and matches the stamped Timestamp value (the interactor passes DryCheckEntry with no recorded_at field — there is no placeholder to ignore); read returns DryCheckReaderError::InvalidData for records where both low_path==high_path AND low_hash==high_hash (self-match — DryCheckPairKey::new returns Err(SelfMatch)), changed_path outside the pair, empty rationale, or empty refactor_proposal on Violation records; GitDryCheckDiffGetter::list_changed_hunks returns Vec<DiffFileHunks> with hunk ranges; FsDryCheckCommitHashStore::read returns Ok(None) on missing file, Err(Format) on invalid hash, Ok(None) on non-ancestor (read() reports precisely; the CLI-composition resolution layer in T007/T009 applies the fail-closed policy: Err(Format) is absorbed with an eprintln! warn and a fall through to git rev-parse main, never propagated as CLI error). cargo make ci-rust passes. (`92aca068`)

### S4 — Usecase layer — interactors for write, read, and gate paths

> Implement the three interactors. DryCheckInteractor (write path): 5-param constructor, NO diff_source param. For each (diff_fragment, candidate_fragment) pair: computes FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of content() for each; constructs FragmentRef::new(path, content_hash); builds DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref, candidate_ref) — Err(SelfMatch) (path AND content_hash both equal) => continue (skip; no string-equality pre-check); Ok(pair_key) => proceed; looks up pair_key in verified set via identifier matching (CN-07: when content changes, FragmentRef changes, DryCheckPairKey changes, no match — no separate hash-comparison step); extracts rationale: Rationale from the judgment variant; maps verdict to DryCheckVerdict (Violation gets RefactorProposal from finding.refactor_proposal().clone()); builds DryCheckEntry::new(pair_key, changed_path, verdict, similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale: Rationale) — 7 arguments, NO recorded_at (interactor does not produce Timestamp); Err(DryCheckEntryError::ChangedPathOutsidePair) -> DryCheckCycleError::Entry; calls DryCheckWriter::append_record(&entry) — infra stamps Timestamp; collects DryCheckFinding (with changed_fragment_ref / candidate_fragment_ref as FragmentRef, refactor_proposal as RefactorProposal) from Violation judgments. No separate low_hash/high_hash computation — content hashes are embedded in the FragmentRefs inside the pair_key.
> DryCheckResultsInteractor (read path): reads full history, derives latest-per-pair by record.pair_key() (the already-sorted DryCheckPairKey with FragmentRefs; no reconstruction), applies VerdictFilter, returns DryCheckResults{records}. Records carry typed Rationale and Timestamp (rationale() -> &Rationale, recorded_at() -> &Timestamp). DryCheckApprovalInteractor (gate path): builds fresh whole-codebase index; for each candidate pair: constructs FragmentRefs from CodeFragment content (SHA-256); builds DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref, candidate_ref) — Err(SelfMatch) => skip (excluded from gate; no PairKey error variant), Ok(pair_key) => proceed; looks up pair_key in latest-per-pair map (key = record.pair_key(); identifier matching — no separate hash-comparison step); if absent: unresolved; if present: checks verdict matches!(DryCheckVerdict::NotAViolation | DryCheckVerdict::Accepted) — Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } is unresolved; returns Approved only when all above-threshold non-self-match pairs for all diff fragments resolve; returns Blocked{unresolved_pair_count} otherwise. Does not record new verdicts.

- [x] **T004**: Implement DryCheckInteractor (DryCheckService write path): constructor new(embedding_port: Arc<dyn EmbeddingPort>, index_port: Arc<dyn SemanticIndexPort>, agent_port: Arc<dyn DryCheckAgentPort>, dry_check_writer: Arc<dyn DryCheckWriter>, dry_check_reader: Arc<dyn DryCheckReader>) — 5 parameters, diff_source is NOT injected. run_dry_check(corpus_fragments: Vec<CodeFragment>, diff_fragments: Vec<CodeFragment>, threshold: SimilarityThreshold, base_commit: CommitHash) -> Result<Vec<DryCheckFinding>, DryCheckCycleError>: the diff_fragments parameter receives only hunk-filtered fragments (pre-filtered by the CLI composition via fragments_overlapping_hunks — CN-04 deterministic, interactor does NOT perform hunk filtering). (1) read existing history via DryCheckReader::read_records to build a BTreeMap<DryCheckPairKey, ()> (verified set) seeded from the latest-per-pair entries: for each CodeFragment in the current run, compute FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of content() -> FragmentRef::new(FilePath(source_path), content_hash); construct DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref, candidate_ref); if the resulting pair_key matches any record's pair_key (identifier matching, CN-07 — when content changes, content_hash changes, so FragmentRef changes, so DryCheckPairKey changes, so no existing record matches; invalidation is implicit in the identifier, no separate hash-comparison step), the pair is already verified. (2) build whole-codebase index from corpus_fragments via EmbeddingPort + SemanticIndexPort; (3) for each diff_fragment, compute changed_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(diff_fragment.source_path.clone()), FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of diff_fragment.content()); run exhaustive threshold-boundary loop (growing k: k, 2k, 4k, ...); stop at threshold boundary, empty batch, or index exhausted; (4) for each candidate in above-threshold prefix: compute candidate_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(candidate.source_path.clone()), FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of candidate.content()); build pair key: match DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref.clone(), candidate_ref) — Err(SelfMatch) (path AND content_hash both equal) => continue (skip — single guard: DryCheckPairKey::new is the sole self-match rejection point; no string-equality pre-check); Ok(pair_key) => proceed; look up pair_key in verified set via identifier matching — skip pairs that match; call DryCheckAgentPort::judge on remaining unverified candidates; receive DryCheckAgentJudgment; (5) for each judgment: extract rationale: Rationale from the judgment variant (all variants carry it — NotAViolation { rationale }, Accepted { rationale }, Violation { rationale, finding }); map verdict from DryCheckAgentJudgment: NotAViolation -> DryCheckVerdict::NotAViolation, Accepted -> DryCheckVerdict::Accepted, Violation { finding, .. } -> DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: finding.refactor_proposal().clone() } (finding.refactor_proposal() returns &RefactorProposal — clone it); changed_path = FilePath(diff_fragment.source_path.clone()) (display-only diff side); build DryCheckEntry::new(pair_key, changed_path, verdict, similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale) — 7 arguments, NO recorded_at; Err(DryCheckEntryError::ChangedPathOutsidePair) propagated as DryCheckCycleError::Entry (internal invariant violation, abort-worthy; asymmetry with skip-on-SelfMatch is intentional; the old Record variant is replaced by Entry); persist via DryCheckWriter::append_record(&entry) — passes DryCheckEntry to the infra adapter which stamps Timestamp internally; add pair_key to verified set; collect DryCheckFinding from Violation judgment's finding field (which carries changed_fragment_ref and candidate_fragment_ref as FragmentRef, and refactor_proposal as RefactorProposal); return Vec<DryCheckFinding>. Unit tests with mock ports: (a) pair cache (by DryCheckPairKey identifier matching) skips already-verified pairs when their FragmentRefs match — same (path, content_hash) pair from prior run is skipped; (b) pair cache invalidated when content changes — new FragmentContentHash produces new FragmentRef produces new DryCheckPairKey that does not match any existing record; (c) self-match candidate (path AND content_hash both equal) excluded via Err(SelfMatch) from DryCheckPairKey::new — agent NOT called; paths-same-hash-different produces a valid pair (complete copies in different files — NOT a self-match); (d) genuine violation found when k grows; (e) NotAViolation returns empty Vec; (f) loop termination on threshold boundary; (g) loop termination on index exhaustion; (h) DryCheckEntry built with 7 fields (pair_key() with low = (path, content_hash)-smaller FragmentRef and high = (path, content_hash)-larger FragmentRef accessible via pair_key().low().path(), pair_key().low().content_hash(), pair_key().high().path(), pair_key().high().content_hash(), changed_path() display-only, verdict: DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal }); (i) rationale from DryCheckAgentJudgment variant is typed Rationale (non-empty newtype); (j) Violation produces DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } AND DryCheckFinding in returned Vec (DryCheckFinding.changed_fragment_ref and .candidate_fragment_ref are FragmentRef, refactor_proposal is RefactorProposal); (k) DryCheckPairKey built from FragmentRefs (path + content_hash), NOT from path-only; (l) no separate low_hash/high_hash fields — content_hash accessible via pair_key().low().content_hash() and pair_key().high().content_hash(). cargo make ci-rust passes. (`eca1f8e0`)
- [x] **T005**: Implement DryCheckResultsInteractor (DryCheckResultsService read path) and DryCheckApprovalInteractor (DryCheckApprovalService gate path). DryCheckResultsInteractor::new(reader: Arc<dyn DryCheckReader>): get_results(filter: VerdictFilter) reads all records via DryCheckReader::read_records, derives the latest-per-pair entries (last occurrence per DryCheckPairKey wins — key = record.pair_key() directly, the already-sorted DryCheckPairKey with FragmentRefs; no reconstruction needed), applies VerdictFilter, returns DryCheckResults{records}. Each record carries all canonical D9 fields (pair_key() (DryCheckPairKey — two FragmentRefs sorted by (path, content_hash)), changed_path() (display-only), verdict() (enum-first DryCheckVerdict: Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } / NotAViolation / Accepted), similarity_score(), threshold(), base_commit(), rationale() -> &Rationale, recorded_at() -> &Timestamp). Violation records carry refactor_proposal as RefactorProposal in verdict via DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal }. DryCheckApprovalInteractor::new(reader, index_port, embedding_port): check_approved(corpus_fragments: Vec<CodeFragment>, diff_fragments: &[CodeFragment], threshold: SimilarityThreshold) -> Result<DryCheckApprovalVerdict, DryCheckCycleError>: builds fresh whole-codebase index from corpus_fragments; reads all records via DryCheckReader::read_records to derive latest-per-pair map (key = record.pair_key(); map value = full DryCheckRecord for verdict check); for each diff_fragment: compute changed_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(diff_fragment.source_path.clone()), FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of diff_fragment.content()); run exhaustive threshold-boundary loop; for each above-threshold candidate: compute candidate_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(candidate.source_path.clone()), FragmentContentHash from SHA-256 of candidate.content()); match DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref.clone(), candidate_ref) — Err(SelfMatch) (path AND content_hash both equal) => skip (excluded from gate; no PairKey error variant), Ok(pair_key) => proceed; look up pair_key in latest-per-pair map via identifier matching (CN-07: if content changes, FragmentContentHash changes, FragmentRef changes, DryCheckPairKey changes, no existing record matches — the pair is unverified; no separate hash-comparison step); if absent: count as unresolved (unverified); if present: check verdict: matches!(stored_record.verdict(), DryCheckVerdict::NotAViolation | DryCheckVerdict::Accepted) — DryCheckVerdict::Violation { .. } counts as unresolved (AC-04/CN-06); returns Approved when all above-threshold non-self-match pairs for all diff fragments resolve; returns Blocked{unresolved_pair_count} otherwise. Unit tests: all-clean returns Approved; one Violation pair (cached verdict=DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal }) returns Blocked; unverified pair returns Blocked; pair invalidated by content change (new FragmentContentHash -> new FragmentRef -> new DryCheckPairKey that does not match any record) returns Blocked; same pair with diff/candidate roles swapped and content unchanged returns Approved (DryCheckPairKey::new sorts both cases to same key); self-match (path AND content_hash both equal) excluded via Err(SelfMatch); paths-same-hash-different (complete copies in different files) is valid pair not excluded; cached NotAViolation with same FragmentRefs returns Approved; cached Accepted with same FragmentRefs returns Approved; get_results(VerdictFilter::All) returns all latest-per-pair records; get_results(VerdictFilter::Violation) returns only DryCheckVerdict::Violation { .. } records; DryCheckResults.records carries full DryCheckRecord entries with pair_key() as DryCheckPairKey (FragmentRef-based), changed_path() display-only, DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } accessible via verdict(), rationale() -> &Rationale, recorded_at() -> &Timestamp. cargo make ci-rust passes. (`4537a104`)

### S5 — Infrastructure layer — CodexDryChecker agent adapter

> Implement CodexDryChecker, the infrastructure adapter for the dry-checker capability. judge(changed_fragment: &CodeFragment, candidate_fragment: &CodeFragment) invokes Codex CLI with --output-schema for structured DryCheckAgentJudgment output (D11). The JSON schema encodes all three struct variants with rationale string on each and Violation's refactor_proposal string — but WITHOUT fragment identity fields: NotAViolation { rationale: String }, Accepted { rationale: String }, Violation { rationale: String, refactor_proposal: String }. The agent does NOT report changed_fragment_ref or candidate_fragment_ref. The adapter constructs Rationale::new(rationale_str) — empty rationale -> DryCheckAgentError::IllegalOutput. For Violation responses, the adapter constructs RefactorProposal::new(refactor_proposal_str) — empty proposal -> DryCheckAgentError::IllegalOutput. The adapter then computes fragment identity from the actual CodeFragment arguments: changed_fragment_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(changed_fragment.source_path()), SHA-256(changed_fragment.content())); candidate_fragment_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(candidate_fragment.source_path()), SHA-256(candidate_fragment.content())); constructs DryCheckFinding::new(changed_fragment_ref, candidate_fragment_ref, refactor_proposal_str). Builds DryCheckAgentJudgment: NotAViolation { rationale: Rationale }, Accepted { rationale: Rationale }, or Violation { rationale: Rationale, finding: DryCheckFinding }. This enforces D8/D9/CN-07: identity/hash data flows from real CodeFragment content, never from LLM output. Empty proposal or empty rationale -> IllegalOutput.
> Maps Codex subprocess outcomes to DryCheckAgentError mirroring CodexReviewer: timed_out -> Timeout; non-zero exit -> AgentAbort; missing/invalid output / empty-rationale / empty-proposal -> IllegalOutput. Sole source of DryCheckAgentJudgment values, enforcing D11 schema-constrained agent output at the infrastructure boundary. Fragment refs (identity) are computed by the adapter from real CodeFragment data — not extracted from agent JSON. Rationale is typed as Rationale newtype; refactor_proposal in Violation is typed as RefactorProposal newtype — both validated non-empty at adapter boundary.

- [~] **T006**: Add infrastructure/dry_check/codex_dry_checker.rs: CodexDryChecker implements DryCheckAgentPort. Constructor: new(model: String, capability_name: String). judge(changed_fragment: &CodeFragment, candidate_fragment: &CodeFragment) -> Result<DryCheckAgentJudgment, DryCheckAgentError>: invokes Codex CLI with --output-schema pointing to the DryCheckAgentJudgment JSON schema (structured output, D11). The JSON schema represents all three struct variants WITHOUT fragment identity fields — the agent reports only verdict and text fields: NotAViolation { rationale: String }, Accepted { rationale: String }, Violation { rationale: String, refactor_proposal: String }. Every parsed response must carry a non-empty rationale string; the adapter constructs Rationale::new(rationale_str) — an empty rationale produces Err(RationaleError::Empty) which maps to DryCheckAgentError::IllegalOutput. For a Violation response, the adapter constructs RefactorProposal::new(refactor_proposal_str) — an empty proposal produces Err(RefactorProposalError::Empty) which maps to DryCheckAgentError::IllegalOutput. The adapter (CodexDryChecker) constructs FragmentRef values from the actual CodeFragment arguments received by judge(): changed_fragment_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(changed_fragment.source_path().clone()), FragmentContentHash computed from SHA-256 of changed_fragment.content()); candidate_fragment_ref = FragmentRef::new(FilePath(candidate_fragment.source_path().clone()), FragmentContentHash computed from SHA-256 of candidate_fragment.content()); then constructs DryCheckFinding::new(changed_fragment_ref, candidate_fragment_ref, refactor_proposal_str) (DryCheckFinding::new calls RefactorProposal::new internally). The agent JSON output does NOT include fragment identity — fragment refs are computed by the adapter from real CodeFragment content, not from agent output (D8/D9/CN-07: identity is always derived from real fragment data, never from LLM output). Builds DryCheckAgentJudgment: for NotAViolation: NotAViolation { rationale: Rationale }; for Accepted: Accepted { rationale: Rationale }; for Violation: Violation { rationale: Rationale, finding: DryCheckFinding }. Maps Codex subprocess outcomes to DryCheckAgentError variants mirroring CodexReviewer adapter: timed_out=true -> Timeout; ProcessFailed (exit_success=false) -> AgentAbort; LastMessageMissing / parse / schema-validation failures including empty-rationale / empty-proposal -> IllegalOutput. Wire into infrastructure::dry_check module. Tests: valid schema JSON with verdict=not-a-violation and non-empty rationale -> NotAViolation { rationale: Rationale }; verdict=violation with non-empty rationale and non-empty proposal -> Violation { rationale: Rationale, finding: DryCheckFinding } (finding.changed_fragment_ref and finding.candidate_fragment_ref are FragmentRef computed by adapter from the CodeFragment arguments NOT from agent JSON, finding.refactor_proposal() returns &RefactorProposal); invalid JSON -> IllegalOutput; empty rationale string -> IllegalOutput; empty refactor_proposal string -> IllegalOutput; exit non-zero -> AgentAbort; timed-out -> Timeout. cargo make ci-rust passes.

### S6 — CLI layer — sotp dry write / results / check-approved subcommands

> Wire all three sotp dry subcommands following the CliApp composition pattern. sotp dry write (T007): --base-commit OPTIONAL; resolve diff base via dry-check-owned FsDryCheckCommitHashStore (CN-01) with fail-closed three-branch policy: Ok(Some(hash)) -> use it; Ok(None) -> main fallback; Err(Format) -> eprintln! warn + main fallback (never CLI abort — malformed .commit_hash must not stop the gate); construct GitDryCheckDiffGetter and call list_changed_hunks(base) for Vec<DiffFileHunks>; apply three-step pipeline (list_changed_hunks -> extract_code_fragments -> fragments_overlapping_hunks -> diff_fragments, CN-04: unchanged fragments structurally absent); corpus_fragments = full workspace (not hunk-filtered); GitDryCheckDiffGetter in cli-composition only, NOT injected into DryCheckInteractor; 5-param DryCheckInteractor constructor; prints DryCheckFinding entries with changed_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash(), candidate_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash(), and refactor_proposal to stdout. sotp dry results (T008): optional --filter flag (default All); calls get_results(filter); prints per-record: pair_key().low().path()/.content_hash() and pair_key().high().path()/.content_hash() (sorted pair identity — path and content_hash together), changed_path() (display-only, labeled), verdict, similarity_score, threshold, base_commit, rationale, recorded_at, and refactor_proposal for Violation records; exits 0 on success regardless of verdicts. sotp dry check-approved (T009): --base-commit OPTIONAL; same dry-check-owned fail-closed diff-base resolution as T007 (Ok(Some) -> use it; Ok(None) -> main fallback; Err(Format) -> warn + main fallback — no CLI abort); three-step pipeline; resolved CommitHash used for diff acquisition but NOT forwarded to check_approved (3-param); blocking gate, exits non-zero on Blocked.
> Exit-code semantics: write exits 0 even with findings (informational for dfl; gate is check-approved); results exits 0 on successful read (informational, historical); check-approved exits non-zero on Blocked (current-scope gate, CN-06/AC-04). Both write and check-approved use identical dry-check-owned diff-base resolution for same-diff scoping.

- [ ] **T007**: Add `sotp dry write` CLI subcommand (write path, D10): parses --track-id / --base-commit (optional) / --db-path / --threshold CLI args. Diff-base resolution and diff acquisition in cli-composition/src/dry.rs: when --base-commit is not given, resolve the diff base via dry-check's OWN FsDryCheckCommitHashStore (CN-01: NOT review_v2's FsCommitHashStore) — construct FsDryCheckCommitHashStore::new(path, trusted_root), call read(). Resolution follows the same three-branch fail-closed policy as review_v2::resolve_diff_base (D4/ADR): Ok(Some(hash)) if valid AND ancestor of HEAD -> use it as base; Ok(None) (file absent or non-ancestor) -> fall back to git rev-parse main; Err(DryCheckCommitHashError::Format) -> emit eprintln! warn and fall back to git rev-parse main (NOT propagated as CLI error; fail-closed — a malformed .commit_hash must not abort the gate). When --base-commit is given, parse it into CommitHash and use it directly (overrides store lookup). Once base CommitHash is resolved, construct GitDryCheckDiffGetter and call list_changed_hunks(base) to obtain Vec<DiffFileHunks> (each carrying FilePath and non-empty Vec<DiffHunkRange>). Then: (1) call extract_code_fragments for changed files to obtain candidate fragments with line spans (start_line/end_line populated by extractor); (2) call fragments_overlapping_hunks(candidate_fragments, &changed_hunks) to filter to only CodeFragments whose [start_line..=end_line] overlaps any DiffHunkRange; (3) use filtered result as diff_fragments (CN-04: unchanged fragments structurally absent). corpus_fragments (whole-codebase index) extracted separately via extract_code_fragments(workspace_root) and NOT filtered by hunks. Construct FsDryCheckStore + CodexDryChecker + semantic-dup ports. Construct DryCheckInteractor::new(embedding_port, index_port, agent_port, dry_check_writer, dry_check_reader) (5-param constructor). Call DryCheckService::run_dry_check(corpus_fragments, diff_fragments, threshold, base_commit). Print summary to stdout: pairs checked, records appended, and for each DryCheckFinding: changed_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash().as_str() (identifying the changed fragment by path and hash), candidate_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash().as_str() (identifying the candidate fragment by path and hash), and refactor_proposal.as_str() (surfacing agent's non-empty RefactorProposal text to dfl per IN-03/AC-03). Exit 0 on success; non-zero on DryCheckCycleError. Wire into cli/commands/dry.rs and apps/cli/src/main.rs command dispatch. Wire composition in cli-composition/src/dry.rs. Unit tests: arg parsing including optional --base-commit, diff-base resolution fallback for Ok(None) (FsDryCheckCommitHashStore + main fallback), diff-base resolution fallback for Err(Format) (warn + main fallback — fail-closed, no CLI abort), CLI-side hunk-scope pipeline (list_changed_hunks -> extract_code_fragments -> fragments_overlapping_hunks) before passing diff_fragments to run_dry_check, corpus_fragments is full workspace. cargo make ci-rust passes.
- [ ] **T008**: Add `sotp dry results` CLI subcommand (informational read path, D10): parses --track-id CLI arg plus optional --filter flag (values: all / not-a-violation / accepted / violation; default: VerdictFilter::All); constructs FsDryCheckStore; calls DryCheckResultsService::get_results(filter); prints records to stdout. For each record displays: the low path (from record.pair_key().low().path()) and high path (from record.pair_key().high().path()) as the sorted pair identity — human-readable repo-relative paths (AC-06/IN-07); low content_hash (from record.pair_key().low().content_hash().as_str()) and high content_hash (from record.pair_key().high().content_hash().as_str()) as the content fingerprints embedded in the pair identity; changed_path (from record.changed_path() — display-only, the diff fragment side at recording time; labeled clearly as display-only); verdict classification (not-a-violation / accepted / violation, from record.verdict()); similarity_score(), threshold(), base_commit(), rationale().as_str() (Rationale newtype, always non-empty), and recorded_at().as_str() (Timestamp newtype — an existing domain::timestamp::Timestamp, not a new type); for violation records, also displays the persisted refactor_proposal.as_str() from DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal: RefactorProposal } (accessed by matching on record.verdict(); RefactorProposal is always non-empty). This command is INFORMATIONAL — it does NOT exit non-zero based on recorded verdicts. Exit 0 on successful read; exit non-zero only on DryCheckReaderError. Wire into cli/commands/dry.rs and cli-composition/src/dry.rs (DryResultsInput struct). Unit tests: successful read exits 0 regardless of recorded verdicts, read error exits non-zero, --filter=violation shows only Violation records, --filter=all shows all latest-per-pair records, output displays record.pair_key().low().path()/.content_hash() and record.pair_key().high().path()/.content_hash() (sorted pair identity), output displays record.changed_path() labeled as display-only, violation record output includes refactor_proposal.as_str(), rationale displayed as Rationale.as_str(), recorded_at displayed as Timestamp.as_str(). cargo make ci-rust passes.
- [ ] **T009**: Add `sotp dry check-approved` CLI subcommand (gate path, D10): parses --track-id / --base-commit (optional) / --db-path / --threshold CLI args. Diff-base resolution and diff acquisition in cli-composition/src/dry.rs: when --base-commit is not given, resolve via dry-check's OWN FsDryCheckCommitHashStore (CN-01) — same three-branch fail-closed policy as T007 (Ok(Some) if valid ancestor -> use it; Ok(None) (absent or non-ancestor) -> fall back to git rev-parse main; Err(DryCheckCommitHashError::Format) -> emit eprintln! warn and fall back to git rev-parse main — NOT propagated as CLI error; fail-closed — a malformed .commit_hash must not abort the gate). When --base-commit is given, parse into CommitHash (overrides store lookup). Once base CommitHash is resolved, construct GitDryCheckDiffGetter and call list_changed_hunks(base) to obtain Vec<DiffFileHunks>. Apply three-step pipeline: (1) extract_code_fragments for changed files with line spans; (2) fragments_overlapping_hunks(candidate_fragments, &changed_hunks) -> diff_fragments; (3) extract corpus_fragments via extract_code_fragments(workspace_root) NOT filtered by hunks. Construct FsDryCheckStore (implements DryCheckReader — identifier-based gate: for each above-threshold non-self-match pair, compute FragmentRefs from CodeFragment content (SHA-256), build DryCheckPairKey::new(changed_ref, candidate_ref); look up in latest-per-pair map; if found and verdict matches!(DryCheckVerdict::NotAViolation | DryCheckVerdict::Accepted): approved pair; if absent or verdict = DryCheckVerdict::Violation { .. }: unresolved) + semantic-dup ports (EmbeddingPort + SemanticIndexPort). Calls DryCheckApprovalService::check_approved(corpus_fragments, diff_fragments, threshold) (3-param — resolved base_commit is used by CLI for diff acquisition but NOT forwarded to check_approved which does not record). Exits 0 on Approved; exits non-zero on Blocked (prints unresolved pair count). Wire into cli/commands/dry.rs and cli-composition/src/dry.rs (DryCheckApprovedInput struct). Unit tests: Approved -> exit 0, Blocked -> exit non-zero, optional --base-commit arg parsing, diff-base resolved via FsDryCheckCommitHashStore when --base-commit absent (Ok(None) -> main fallback), malformed .commit_hash (Err(Format)) -> warn + main fallback (fail-closed, no CLI abort), CLI-side hunk-scope pipeline, corpus_fragments is full workspace. cargo make ci-rust passes.

### S7 — Configuration and workflow — capability registration and DFP/RFP phase docs

> Register dry-checker and dry-fix-lead as separate capability entries in .harness/config/agent-profiles.json (AC-01, CN-03). Update workflow command docs to document the two-phase DFP->RFP ordering; document that sotp dry write surfaces DryCheckFinding refactor proposals (each finding carries changed_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash() and candidate_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash() as the full (path, content_hash) identifiers of the two fragments, and refactor_proposal for dfl); clarify distinction between sotp dry results (informational, historical — pair identity displayed as low_path, low content_hash, high_path, high content_hash from pair_key().low()/.high(); changed_path() display-only; refactor_proposal for Violation from DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal }; exit 0 on success) and sotp dry check-approved (authoritative current-scope gate, exit non-zero on Blocked). Document CN-01 enforced end-to-end. Document the canonical (path, content_hash) FragmentRef identity design: pair identity = DryCheckPairKey holding two FragmentRefs sorted by (path, content_hash); self-match = both path AND content_hash equal (paths-different-hash-same = valid pair not a self-match); invalidation identifier-based (CN-07): content change -> hash change -> FragmentRef change -> DryCheckPairKey change -> no record match; on-disk: four flat fields (low_path/low_hash/high_path/high_hash); changed_path DISPLAY-ONLY. DFP gate-pass before RFP; rfl halts on DRY violation.
> Configuration-only task. Completes agent capability registration (D3) and codifies two-phase sequencing (D2/D12/D13) and CN-01 contract in workflow documentation.

- [ ] **T010**: Register new capabilities and update workflow configuration: (1) add `dry-checker` capability entry to .harness/config/agent-profiles.json with provider and model (mirroring reviewer pattern); (2) add `dry-fix-lead` (dfl) capability entry as a separate entry distinct from `review-fix-lead` (rfl); (3) update workflow command docs (.claude/commands/track/ or DEVELOPER_AI_WORKFLOW.md) to document the DFP (dfl, whole-codebase, sotp dry write -> sotp dry check-approved) -> RFP (rfl, per-scope parallel) two-phase ordering, RFP halt-to-DFP back-edge, and fixpoint commit gate (DRY gate + all review scopes green); (4) document that sotp dry write surfaces DryCheckFinding refactor proposals for dfl (each finding carries changed_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash() and candidate_fragment_ref.path()/.content_hash() as FragmentRef identifiers, and refactor_proposal); (5) document that sotp dry results is informational (recorded/historical state with optional --filter; displays pair_key().low().path()/.content_hash() and pair_key().high().path()/.content_hash() as the sorted pair identity — path and content_hash together form the identifier; displays changed_path() as the display-only diff fragment side path; displays refactor_proposal for Violation records from DryCheckVerdict::Violation { refactor_proposal }; exit 0 on success) and sotp dry check-approved is the authoritative current-scope gate; (6) document that both sotp dry write and sotp dry check-approved resolve diff base via dry-check-owned FsDryCheckCommitHashStore (NOT review_v2's FsCommitHashStore) + ancestor check + main fallback, --base-commit as optional override; (7) document CN-01 enforcement end-to-end: DryCheckDiffSource/GitDryCheckDiffGetter and FsDryCheckCommitHashStore are dry-check-owned adapters independent from review_v2's DiffGetter/FsCommitHashStore/resolve_diff_base; GitDryCheckDiffGetter is wired in CLI composition only and NOT injected into interactors; (8) document the canonical (path, content_hash) FragmentRef identity design: each DryCheckPairKey holds two FragmentRefs ({path: FilePath, content_hash: FragmentContentHash}) sorted lexicographically by (path, content_hash) into (low, high); DryCheckPairKey::new rejects self-match when BOTH path AND content_hash are equal ('paths different but hashes same' = complete copies in different files = valid pair); invalidation is identifier-based (CN-07): when content changes, content_hash changes, FragmentRef changes, DryCheckPairKey changes, no existing record matches — no separate hash-comparison step; on-disk schema stores pair as flat four fields (low_path/low_hash/high_path/high_hash); changed_path is DISPLAY-ONLY (diff fragment side at recording time); self-matches (path AND content_hash both equal after sorting) never recorded. cargo make ci passes (verify-* checks include agent-profiles.json validation).
